title: Homer Vs Dignity

jokes.text: Happy first 'A' Bart Simpson
jokes.setup: Celebratory banner for academic achievement
jokes.punchline: The irony of celebrating Bart's FIRST A-grade as a major milestone
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor highlighting Bart's poor academic record

jokes.text: Now that our son is an honor student... I'm going to get one of those bumper stickers... that informs strangers of that fact.
jokes.setup: Bart gets his first A, family celebrating
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest desire to broadcast Bart's achievement to strangers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment - her sincere suburban pride in the most mundane achievement

jokes.text: Just a cotton-pickin' minute. I've been getting A's since Gymboree.
jokes.setup: Parents celebrating Bart's first A
jokes.punchline: Lisa's indignation at being overlooked despite consistent excellence
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa - both the vocabulary ('cotton-pickin'') and the concept (achieving since infancy) perfectly capture her character

jokes.text: I was trying to breed the hamster with the lizard... to create an unholy supercreature...
jokes.setup: Bart explaining how he got motivated to study
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of attempting to create genetic abominations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart moment - the phrase 'unholy supercreature' is both unexpected and perfectly in character for his mischievous nature

jokes.text: when I saw an even worse crime against nature.
jokes.setup: Bart talking about creating unholy supercreature
jokes.punchline: Something even worse - cuts to Skinner and Krabappel making out
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up genetic experimentation as the 'crime against nature' then reveals something even more disturbing to a 10-year-old

jokes.text: While the kids are out getting their nits picked, we can have our own private 'cootie call.'
jokes.setup: Head lice inspection day at school
jokes.punchline: Skinner's terrible pun combining 'booty call' with 'cooties'
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey for Skinner - the pun is deliberately bad and the situation uncomfortable

jokes.text: Oh, you talk too much. Let's do it on Martin's desk. - It is usually the cleanest.
jokes.setup: Teachers about to get intimate
jokes.punchline: They choose Martin's desk because it's cleanest
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - Martin would indeed have the cleanest desk, and it's both practical and disturbing

jokes.text: It was like a whole different kind of cheating.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining how studying helped him remember answers
jokes.punchline: He considers learning to be a form of cheating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Bart's warped perspective makes legitimate studying seem like cheating. Perfect economy and character insight.

jokes.text: [Computerized Male Voice] Deadbeat. Deadbeat. Deadbeat.
jokes.setup: Homer's credit card rejected at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Machine publicly shaming him with repeated 'Deadbeat'
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept - the repetition and public humiliation escalates the embarrassment

jokes.text: They're getting away / Stop them, Mary Kay
jokes.setup: Family running away from restaurant without paying
jokes.punchline: Staff singing chase instructions to mariachi music
jokes.explanation: Decent absurd moment but not particularly memorable or character-specific

jokes.text: Your playing, while technically proficient, lacks passion.
jokes.setup: Homer forced to play mariachi music to pay for meal
jokes.punchline: Patron gives serious musical criticism
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast - treating Homer's desperate busking as a serious musical performance worthy of critique

jokes.text: I think it was when that cold snap killed off all the hoboes.
jokes.setup: Homer asking when they became bottom rung of society
jokes.punchline: Marge matter-of-factly references hobo genocide
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected from Marge. The casual delivery of such a grim concept is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I had to sell it for gas money... Which I spent on a novelty horn.
jokes.setup: Kids asking about missing backseat
jokes.punchline: Homer sold it for gas money then wasted that money on something frivolous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - the complete lack of financial sense followed by the horn sound effect seals it

jokes.text: Financial panther, eh?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests talking to a financial planner
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears as 'financial panther'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mishearing - sets up the visual gag of Burns having an actual panther

jokes.text: Get him, Sheba!
jokes.setup: Homer mishearing 'financial planner' as 'financial panther'
jokes.punchline: The planner actually has a panther that attacks Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual payoff to Homer's mishearing - absurd that a financial planner would have a trained panther

jokes.text: You know how it is with cops. I'll get shot three days before retirement.
jokes.setup: Planner asking about retirement planning
jokes.punchline: Homer references tired cop movie cliché as if it's inevitable
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about movie tropes, delivered with Homer's characteristic certainty about unlikely scenarios

jokes.text: In the business we call it 'retirony.'
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning getting shot before retirement
jokes.punchline: Planner has a professional term combining 'retirement' and 'irony'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - 'retirony' is both clever and feels like something professionals would actually coin

jokes.text: What a terrible thing to say. Now, look. You made my wife cry.
jokes.setup: Planner suggesting Homer might not get shot
jokes.punchline: Homer acts like NOT getting shot is offensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer illogic - being told he might survive is somehow insulting to him

jokes.text: Just give the word, and I'll cut him off. - I couldn't ask you to do that. - Consider it done.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer supporting his father
jokes.punchline: Homer instantly abandons Grampa at first suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selfishness and poor impulse control

jokes.text: Marge, your posture looks terrible.
jokes.setup: Financial planner showing alarming projection chart
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on completely irrelevant detail about Marge
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer attention deficit - completely missing the financial disaster to focus on posture

jokes.text: your intelligence profile indicates that you're too... 'stupid' to stick to a budget. - Yes. Go on.
jokes.setup: Planner delivering harsh financial assessment
jokes.punchline: Homer encourages him to continue after being called stupid
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his complete lack of awareness of being insulted

jokes.text: I'm a financial planner, not a financial consultant.
jokes.setup: Homer asking how to get more money
jokes.punchline: Planner makes meaningless professional distinction to avoid helping
jokes.explanation: Decent joke about professional buck-passing and meaningless job title distinctions

jokes.text: I know you're not a deaf-mute, Mr. Simpson. We've been talking for the last 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably trying to fake being deaf-mute to avoid paying
jokes.punchline: Planner points out the obvious flaw in this strategy
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's desperation and poor planning skills

jokes.text: Why not write a musical about the common cat or the king of Siam?
jokes.setup: Smithers mentioning his Malibu Stacy musical
jokes.punchline: Burns sarcastically suggests ideas that are actually famous musicals
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to 'Cats' and 'The King and I' - Burns unknowingly suggests hugely successful musicals

jokes.text: Whoa. Whoa. Slow down there, maestro. There's a new Mexico?
jokes.setup: Smithers mentioning theater in New Mexico
jokes.punchline: Burns is shocked to learn New Mexico exists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - his extreme isolation and age manifesting as basic geographical ignorance

jokes.text: Well, with the old ball and chain gone, maybe I can finally have a little fun at the office.
jokes.setup: Smithers leaving Burns alone
jokes.punchline: Burns referring to Smithers as his 'ball and chain'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' dependence on Smithers and obliviousness to the phrase's implications

jokes.text: You've made a powerful enemy today, my friend.
jokes.setup: Burns buying candy, presumably having minor interaction with clerk
jokes.punchline: Burns declaring war over trivial candy shop transaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns overreaction - treating minor slight as grounds for vendetta

jokes.text: Oh, I... concur. Word-a-day Calendar.
jokes.setup: Someone using word 'concur'
jokes.punchline: Homer admits his vocabulary source
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail about Homer trying to sound smart but being transparent about it

jokes.text: This must be some sort of cafetorium.
jokes.setup: Burns entering cafeteria
jokes.punchline: Burns using made-up word 'cafetorium'
jokes.explanation: Decent Burns vocabulary moment - combining 'cafeteria' and 'auditorium'

jokes.text: That's the sneeze guard. You have to lean under it to get salad or sneeze on stuff.
jokes.setup: Burns confused by sneeze guard
jokes.punchline: Homer explains its dual purpose including contamination
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about Homer misunderstanding sneeze guard purpose

jokes.text: Well, sir-You see, sir, I've worked here a long time... and my wife has a game leg. And my kids have game things as well.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Burns for raise
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly vague and nonsensical justification
jokes.explanation: Good Homer rambling - starts with real excuse then deteriorates into meaninglessness

jokes.text: I'm a bored and joyless old man. Give me a 'larf.'
jokes.setup: Homer asking for raise
jokes.punchline: Burns admitting his existential emptiness and mispronouncing 'laugh'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - both pathetic and imperious, with his characteristic old-fashioned pronunciation

jokes.text: At Lenny? But he's a war hero. - Well, let's decorate him then.
jokes.setup: Burns wanting Homer to throw pudding at Lenny
jokes.punchline: Burns' dark pun on 'decorate' (military honors vs. food splatter)
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark wordplay - Burns twists military decoration into literal decoration with food

jokes.text: Ow! My eye! I'm not supposed to get pudding in it!
jokes.setup: Lenny getting hit with pudding
jokes.punchline: Lenny's oddly specific medical restriction
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail - suggests Lenny has very specific medical condition involving pudding

jokes.text: What say I make you my executive in charge of recreation? No. No. Better yet, my prank monkey.
jokes.setup: Burns pleased with Homer's pudding throwing
jokes.punchline: Burns upgrades title from executive to 'prank monkey'
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment - thinks 'prank monkey' is an upgrade, shows his twisted values

jokes.text: I can't have my little monkey running around in rags.
jokes.setup: Homer accepting prank monkey job
jokes.punchline: Burns treating Homer like actual pet monkey
jokes.explanation: Sets up the degrading relationship - Burns literally treating Homer as subhuman

jokes.text: Oh, if only the real chicks went down this easy.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy playing arcade game
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment about video game
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy creepy comment - in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Look at that comic book fellow calmly eating candy like a Spaniard.
jokes.setup: Burns observing Comic Book Guy
jokes.punchline: Burns' bizarre cultural stereotype about Spanish candy eating
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random Burns observation - completely nonsensical cultural reference that sounds authoritative

jokes.text: And I'd like an hour on the holodeck with Seven of Nine.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy responding to Homer's request for comic
jokes.punchline: CBG makes Star Trek reference about wanting time with attractive character
jokes.explanation: Standard Comic Book Guy nerd reference - in character but predictable

jokes.text: Oh! No! What are you doing? Good, fair, poor!
jokes.setup: Homer about to eat rare comic book
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy desperately grading the comic's condition as it's destroyed
jokes.explanation: Excellent Comic Book Guy moment - his obsession with condition grading continues even during destruction

jokes.text: Uh, no, thanks. I'll just eat it here.
jokes.setup: Comic book store owner offers bagging for valuable comic
jokes.punchline: Homer casually announces intention to eat priceless collectible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - treats collectible comic like candy bar

jokes.text: Good, fair, poor! [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Homer eating the valuable comic book
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy rates the comic's condition as it's destroyed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Comic Book Guy's compulsive grading even during tragedy

jokes.text: There. [Whimpers] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer expects payment after comic book prank
jokes.punchline: Burns gives him tiny amount, Homer's disappointed reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple payoff gag - Homer learning Burns pays poorly

jokes.text: Why, he doesn't know the meaning of the word 'gay.'
jokes.setup: Burns comparing Homer favorably to Smithers
jokes.punchline: Burns' obliviousness to Smithers' sexuality despite obvious hints
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Burns completely missing Smithers' obvious homosexuality despite years of evidence

jokes.text: I made a boom-boom.
jokes.setup: Homer in bathroom stall at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Homer talking like a toddler about using bathroom
jokes.explanation: Low-brow bathroom humor - gets reaction but relies on cheap scatological comedy

jokes.text: Baby made a boom-boom.
jokes.setup: Homer already embarrassing himself in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Homer repeats baby talk to shocked restaurant patrons
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bathroom humor but still relies on cheap shock value

jokes.text: Business stuff. Uh, downsizing... e-solutions, the glass ceiling.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Homer's work for Burns
jokes.punchline: Homer using meaningless corporate buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate speak - Homer randomly throwing out buzzwords to sound legitimate

jokes.text: What do you do with the $68 I send your mother every month? - Weekday Dad wanted a DVD player.
jokes.setup: Grampa asking Homer for money
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Weekday Dad' wanting expensive electronics
jokes.explanation: Intriguing reference to 'Weekday Dad' - suggests Grampa has some kind of split personality or routine

jokes.text: Why do I have to do the Lindy Hop?
jokes.setup: Homer being prepared to pretend to be panda
jokes.punchline: Homer questioning why panda would do 1920s dance
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail - why would a panda know a specific vintage dance move?

jokes.text: That's it. Twenty-three skiddoo, 24... 25 skiddoo.
jokes.setup: Homer doing Lindy Hop as panda
jokes.punchline: Homer counting off with vintage slang
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about Homer using period-appropriate slang while in panda suit

jokes.text: pandas only feel the slightest tickle from these powerful electric shocks.
jokes.setup: Homer being shocked with cattle prods
jokes.punchline: Narrator's obvious lie about pandas not feeling pain
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - obvious cover-up for animal abuse while Homer screams in pain

jokes.text: Ohh. She's saying 'I love you.'
jokes.setup: Homer screaming in agony from electric shocks
jokes.punchline: Narrator interpreting screams as expressions of love
jokes.explanation: Continues the narrator's absurd misinterpretation of obvious torture

jokes.text: Hey, there's our resident bull panda, Ping Ping. With any luck, folks, these two will become very, very close, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Real panda enters Homer's enclosure
jokes.punchline: Announcer suggests romantic/sexual encounter to family audience
jokes.explanation: Adult innuendo in family setting - typical Simpsons inappropriate humor

jokes.text: Oh, look. She's presenting. We thought this would take years to happen.
jokes.setup: Homer being attacked by male panda
jokes.punchline: Narrator interpreting assault as mating behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues narrator's misinterpretation theme but getting more disturbing and less funny

jokes.text: Ping Ping just asked Sim Sim to marry him... and I think she just said yes.
jokes.setup: Homer being mauled by male panda
jokes.punchline: Narrator describing violence as romantic proposal
jokes.explanation: Escalates the narrator bit but the joke is wearing thin and becoming uncomfortable

jokes.text: Dad? The panda was you? Did you see Daddy dance? They all saw me dance.
jokes.setup: Kids discover Homer was the fake panda
jokes.punchline: Homer's mortification about public dancing rather than the deception
jokes.explanation: Good Homer priorities - more embarrassed by dancing than elaborate deception

jokes.text: Yes. I humiliate myself for fistfuls of cash.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Homer's mysterious job
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest summary of his situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely honest about his degradation while sobbing

jokes.text: That other panda gave some unexpected 'zazz to the festivities, eh?
jokes.setup: Homer nearly killed by real panda
jokes.punchline: Burns treating Homer's near-death as entertainment enhancement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns callousness - using jazz-age slang 'zazz' to describe Homer's trauma

jokes.text: Too busy trying to keep my sides from splitting. It's happened before.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why Burns didn't rescue him
jokes.punchline: Burns literally split his sides from laughing before
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Burns moment - his advanced age makes literal 'splitting sides' plausible and horrifying

jokes.text: Oh, shut up, you tub of guts.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for respect
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately proving Homer's point with insult
jokes.explanation: Good timing - Burns immediately demonstrates the disrespect Homer just complained about

jokes.text: Yes, I may be naked and reeking of panda love... but I've got to stop this before it goes too far.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to quit being Burns' prank monkey
jokes.punchline: Homer's graphic description of his current state
jokes.explanation: Memorable gross-out line that captures Homer's degraded state - 'reeking of panda love' is particularly vivid

jokes.text: Take that back... for $903.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically rejecting Burns' money
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately selling out his principles for specific amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - undermines his moral stand immediately with oddly specific price point

jokes.text: Well, well. It looks like my monkey has evolved into a man. A poor man.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting Burns' money and standing up to him
jokes.punchline: Burns' cruel parting shot about Homer's poverty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns cruelty - acknowledges Homer's growth then immediately crushes it with economic reality

jokes.text: Wha- Oh, why did he have to say that extra thing?
jokes.setup: Burns delivering cutting insult about Homer being poor
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated reaction to Burns' unnecessary cruelty
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer recognizes Burns didn't need to twist the knife but did anyway

jokes.text: I see what you're saying. I need to buy a gun.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Homer help needy children
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misunderstanding the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur - completely misinterprets charitable suggestion as call to violence

jokes.text: I've had a bit of a shoe-sniffing problem myself.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning digging out of pit of shame
jokes.punchline: Costington casually admitting to bizarre fetish
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected character detail - Costington's casual admission of strange compulsion

jokes.text: I'm still not allowed on the third floor.
jokes.setup: Costington admitting shoe-sniffing problem
jokes.punchline: Revealing ongoing consequences of his behavior
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing the shoe-sniffing had serious consequences

jokes.text: the thing downtown that's open... right next to the men's shelter.
jokes.setup: TV broadcast about Costington's department store
jokes.punchline: Kent's brutally honest description of the store's location and appeal
jokes.explanation: Good Kent Brockman moment - his casual dismissal of local business

jokes.text: 'Rusty' the Clown? Ah, Springfield gets the lamest balloons. Are you kidding? There's Funky Winkerbean!
jokes.setup: Bart complains about lame parade balloons
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out comic strip character balloon as if it's exciting
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about obscure comic strip characters in parades

jokes.text: Over here, Funky! - Look, it's the Noid. Avoid the Noid. He ruins pizzas.
jokes.setup: Kids watching Thanksgiving parade with lame balloons
jokes.punchline: References to obscure 1980s cultural figures
jokes.explanation: Decent nostalgia humor - Funky Winkerbean and the Domino's Noid are dated references

jokes.text: The papier-mâché is composed entirely of broken treaties.
jokes.setup: Float celebrating Native Americans
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning it's made from symbols of betrayal
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark historical humor - perfectly captures American hypocrisy about Native American treatment

jokes.text: Not for one million dollars? [Gasps] Oh, so much money. But Santa can't be evil. But it's so much money.
jokes.setup: Burns offers Homer a million dollars to ruin Christmas
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal struggle between morality and greed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moral dilemma - torn between conscience and massive bribe

jokes.text: Wait a minute, Kent. That is not candy. That's fish guts.
jokes.setup: Homer as Santa throwing things to crowd
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's throwing disgusting fish parts
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out escalation but relies on simple shock value

jokes.text: Oh! Why, Santa? Why?
jokes.setup: Moe getting hit with fish guts from fake Santa
jokes.punchline: Moe's existential crisis directed at Santa
jokes.explanation: Good Moe moment - his despair is both funny and touching, questioning Santa's motives

jokes.text: But if you're here, who's that on the float? Ho, ho, ho! Merry 'Fishmas'!
jokes.setup: Lisa realizes Homer isn't on the float
jokes.punchline: Burns reveals he's taken over Santa duties with fish-themed greeting
jokes.explanation: Excellent climax - Burns personally executing his evil plan with perfect pun

jokes.text: Ho, ho, ho! Merry 'Fishmas'!
jokes.setup: Burns taking over as fake Santa throwing fish guts
jokes.punchline: Burns' portmanteau of 'Fish' and 'Christmas'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that captures Burns' cruel glee at ruining Christmas with fish

jokes.text: Let's just say Lisa gave me an early Christmas present: The gift of dignity.
jokes.setup: Family asks how Homer regained his dignity
jokes.punchline: Credits Lisa with giving him the gift of self-respect
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending - Homer acknowledging Lisa's moral influence

jokes.text: Shh! [Homer] Baby made a boom-boom.
jokes.setup: End credits scene
jokes.punchline: Homer still stuck in bathroom situation from earlier
jokes.explanation: Simple tag ending callback to earlier embarrassing moment

tmdb_overview: When the Simpsons once again have financial problems, Mr. Burns pays Homer to play pranks on others and humiliate himself in public. Meanwhile, Smithers creates a Malibu Stacy-themed musical.
